summary: Routed 8 interpolated user-facing copy sites through NSLocalizedString + String.localizedStringWithFormat. Added 6 new format-string keys across all 19 locales (LLM-drafted, not native-reviewed). TrainView gained a private trainingReadinessScaleAccessibilityLabel helper that reuses the existing today.readiness.scale.accessibility stringsdict keys to avoid duplicating plural-aware accessibility data. Build green, parity 19/330/13. The TextField('Step 1\nStep 2\nStep 3', ...) placeholders in HomeView were verified already-localizing via the existing literal-key route, so left untouched. Pure-number interpolations and developer fields were left as-is per slice boundary.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-interp-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
missing_proof_levels: running-app-smoke for translated content per locale

contract_status_changes.before: 8 interpolation sites in TodayView/HomeView/TrainView mixed English copy with runtime values: 'Next: <activity>', 'Active protocol: <title>', 'Next task: <title>', '<n>/<m> completed', '<n> of <m>', '<label> <level> of 5', '<label>, <value> of 5'. The English fragments were displayed verbatim to all locales.
contract_status_changes.after: All 8 sites use String.localizedStringWithFormat with new keys (or the existing today.readiness.scale.accessibility stringsdict for the Train readiness scale). Format-string positional args (%1$d, %2$d) used where multiple counts appear in one line, allowing per-language reordering.

residual_risks: All 6 new keys are LLM-drafted by claude-opus-4-7, not native-reviewed. Subtle wording/punctuation errors are possible. The colon/separator choices may not match locale conventions (e.g., Arabic might prefer different punctuation than ':').

home.protocol.steps.placeholder was added across 19 locales but is not currently wired into any call site (the existing TextField('Step 1\nStep 2\nStep 3', ...) route works via the existing literal-key). The unused key is reserved; can be wired if the literal-key approach ever stops working.

TrainView's TrainingReadinessScaleRow now reuses today.readiness.scale.accessibility stringsdict keys. If those keys are renamed in the future, TrainView accessibility breaks too. Cross-feature coupling is intentional but worth noting.

The %1$d/%2$d positional format style assumes the language's number conventions allow direct digit substitution. Right-to-left locales (Arabic) may need additional review for whether the number order should swap in the visible string. Currently the format keeps left-first numeral.

Pure-number interpolations were intentionally left as-is (\(count), \(value), \(staleDayCount)d). The 'd' suffix on the day counter remains hardcoded English; that's a minor compact-format issue, separate slice if needed.
BuildInfoView developer field interpolation '\(label): \(value)' remains as-is per slice boundary.

AudioPlayerService inner error messages ('Recording not found.', 'Could not play recording.') remain hardcoded English. They route into the now-localized 'Playback error: %@' wrapper but the inner content is still untranslated. Separate slice if needed.
Per-locale review return files snapshot 356 entries from 2026-05-15; the 6 new keys are NOT reflected. STATUS.md / LQA.md show stale counts. Housekeeping task.
Native review remains outstanding for every locale.
recommended_next_reason: All open NLS implementation slices are complete. Remaining queued items: only app-localization-native-review-intake (blocked on human-supplied review). Clean stop on actionable NLS work.
